element_introduction_order.element: Basic island governance and building placement
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns game mechanics and controls in guided scenario
Individual factions can disrupt or end El Presidente's rule; requires balancing approval
Cold War superpowers can provide foreign aid, build military bases, or launch invasions based on satisfaction
Player must maintain island economy health; tax breaks and edicts affect citizen happiness and treasury
Army can stage coup d'état; rebels can defeat army and storm palace if dissatisfied
Player must maintain positive populace approval or face removal through voting; electoral fraud possible
Complex edicts with funding requirements; range from praise to superpowers to martial law declaration
Swiss bank account accumulation affects final score but creates tension with populace needs

typical_tension_triggers: Army dissatisfaction escalating to coup risk
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-form tension with variable cycles; players can customize game length (10-70 years simulated); tension builds across election cycles and superpower satisfaction arcs; relief comes through successful political manipulation and economic stabilization

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Map customization (size and steepness) affects gameplay challenge; allows player agency in spatial design

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial teaches basic mechanics; initial scenarios establish foundational governance tasks with manageable faction/superpower pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Political complexity increases; faction management becomes critical; superpower involvement escalates; military coup risk emerges
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multiple competing pressures: election maintenance, faction balance, superpower appeasement, economic health, military loyalty; endgame score factors reward long-term planning

notable_level_patterns.description: Predetermined game conditions with defined victory objectives; each scenario has stated difficulty level; teaches specific mechanics sequentially
Players can customize starting conditions, map properties, political/economic difficulty, simulation length (10-70 years), and victory conditions; enables replayability and difficulty scaling
Superpowers (US/USSR) provide aid or threaten invasion; can establish military bases on island; creates ongoing tension management distinct from domestic politics
Player simultaneously manages: populace voting approval, faction satisfaction, superpower relations, army loyalty, rebel activity, and economic health; no single dominant threat
